data.title: TransAlta FFO of C$0.47, revenue of C$758M; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• TransAlta press release (NYSE:TAC): Q1 Revenue of C$758M (-20.0% Y/Y).
  • Achieved strong operational availability of 94.9 per cent in 2025, compared to 92.3 per cent in 2024
  • Adjusted EBITDA of C$270 million, compared to C$342 million for the same period in 2024
  • Free Cash Flow of C$139 million, or C$0.47 per share, compared to C$221 million, or C$0.72 per share, for the same period in 2024
  • Adjusted earnings before income taxes(1) of C$28 million, or C$0.09 per share, compared to C$144 million, or C$0.47 per share, for the same period in 2024
  • Cash flow from operating activities of C$7 million, compared to C$244 million from the same period in 2024
  • Net earnings attributable to common shareholders of C$46 million, or C$0.15 per share, compared to C$222 million, or C$0.72 per share, for the same period in 2024.
  • The Company remains confident in its ability to achieve results within its previously stated guidance range. 

  • 2025 Outlook

    We remain confident in our ability to meet our 2025 Outlook.

    The following table outlines our expectations on key financial targets and related assumptions for 2025 and should be read in conjunction with the narrative discussion that follows and the Governance and Risk Management section of TransAlta's first quarter 2025 MD&A for additional information:

    Measure 2025 Target
    Adjusted EBITDA C$1,150 to C$1,250 million
    FCF C$450 to C$550 million
    FCF per share C$1.51 to C$1.85
    Annual dividend per share C$0.26 annualized

    The Company's outlook for 2025 may be impacted by a number of factors as detailed below.

    Market 2025 Assumptions
    Alberta spot ($/MWh) C$40 to C$60
    Mid-Columbia spot (US$/MWh) US$50 to US$70
    AECO gas price ($/GJ) C$1.60 to C$2.10

    Alberta spot price sensitivity: a +/- $1 per MWh change in spot price is expected to have a +/-$2 million impact on adjusted EBITDA for the balance of the year.

    Other assumptions relevant to the 2025 outlook

      2025 Assumptions
    Energy Marketing gross margin C$110 to C$130 million
    Sustaining capital C$145 to C$165 million
    Current income tax expense C$95 to C$130 million
    Net interest expense C$255 to C$275 million

data.title: Avista GAAP EPS of $0.98 misses by $0.05, revenue of $603M misses by $28.85M
data.description:
• Avista press release (NYSE:AVA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.98 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $603M (+1.4% Y/Y) misses by $28.85M.

  • As of Mar. 31, 2025, we had $221 million of available liquidity under the Avista Corp. committed line of credit and $40 million of available liquidity under our letter of credit facility. AEL&P had $12 million available under their line of credit as of Mar. 31, 2025.

    In 2025, we expect to issue $120 million of long-term debt and up to $80 million of common stock, including $16 million issued in the first quarter.

  • In the first quarter of 2025, Avista Utilities' capital expenditures were $100 million and AEL&P's capital expenditures were $3 million.

    For Avista Utilities, we expect capital expenditures to be about $525 million in 2025. For the five-year period ending in 2029, we expect total capital expenditures at Avista Utilities of nearly $3 billion, resulting in an annual growth rate of 5 to 6 percent. These estimates do not include potential expenditures for additional generation from our all source Request for Proposal, transmission projects or new large load customers.

    Capital expenditures at AEL&P are expected to be $15 million in 2025.

    In addition, we expect to invest $10 million in 2025 at our other businesses related to non-regulated investment opportunities and economic development projects in our service territory.

data.title: CEVA Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.01, revenue of $24.25M misses by $2.3M
data.description:
• CEVA press release (NASDAQ:CEVA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $24.25M (+9.7% Y/Y) misses by $2.3M.
  • 11 license agreements concluded in the quarter, including Wi-Fi 7 deal with long-term strategic connectivity customer, edge AI NPU for automotive ADAS and spatial audio software for wireless headsets for a leading PC OEM
  • Powered 420 million devices in the quarter, up 13% year-over-year
  • Customer milestone: leading U.S. OEM successfully ramped in-house 5G modem integrating Ceva IP

data.title: Bruker Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.03, revenue of $801.4M beats by $28.93M
data.description:
• Bruker press release (NASDAQ:BRKR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $801.4M (+11.0% Y/Y) beats by $28.93M.
  •  Q1 2025 revenues of $801.4 million, up 11.0% year-over-year; organic revenue up 2.9%, and constant-exchange rate (CER) revenue up 12.5%; Bruker Scientific Instrument (BSI) segment organic revenue up 5.1%
  • Guidance on organic revenue and EPS adjusted for US policy changes and tariff headwinds. Updated FY 2025 guidance:
    • Revenue $3.48 to $3.55 billion, up 3.5% to 5.5%, incl. organic growth of 0% to 2%
    • Non-GAAP EPS $2.40 to $2.48, growth of 0% to 3% yoy

data.title: Disney beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Disney press release (NYSE:DIS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.24.
  • Revenue of $23.6B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $470M.
  • Direct-to-Consumer operating income increased $289 million to $336 million
  • 180.7 million Disney+ and Hulu subscriptions, an increase of 2.5 million versus Q1 fiscal 2025
  • 126.0 million Disney+ subscribers, an increase of 1.4 million versus Q1 fiscal 2025
  • Linear Networks operating income grew 2%; year-over-year growth includes a comparison to $89 million of operating income in Q2 fiscal 2024 from Star India

  • Guidance and Outlook:

    • Q3 Fiscal 2025:
      • Entertainment Direct-to-Consumer: Modest increase in Disney+ subscribers compared to Q2 fiscal 2025
    • Fiscal Year 2025:
      • Adjusted EPS(1) of $5.75, an increase of 16% over fiscal 2024 vs. consnesus of $5.44 (prior High-single digit adjusted EPS growth compared to fiscal 2024)
      • Cash provided by operations of $17 billion, a $2 billion increase over prior guidance driven by the deferral of tax payments
      • Entertainment: Double-digit percentage segment operating income growth
      • Sports: 18% segment operating income growth
      • Experiences: 6% to 8% segment operating income growth
      • Disney Cruise Line pre-opening expense of ~$200 million, with ~$40 million in Q3 and ~$50 million in Q4
      • Equity loss from India JV of ~$300 million driven by purchase accounting amortization
